title: Parasite
notes: Absolutely floored by this. The basement reveal is one of the best moments in cinema. Rewatched it immediately.

title: The Lighthouse
notes: Weird and unsettling in the best way. Willem Dafoe's monologue is something else.

title: Knives Out
notes: Pure fun. The kind of movie that reminds you why you love movies. Great ensemble cast.

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
notes: Cried three times. Somehow a movie about a laundromat tax audit is the most emotionally resonant film I've seen in years.

title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
notes: Peak Wes Anderson. Rewatched probably 4 times. Ralph Fiennes is perfect.

title: Hereditary
notes: Genuinely disturbing. The dinner table scene. I think about it still.

title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
notes: One of the most visually beautiful films I've ever seen. Slow but never boring.

title: Arrival
notes: The twist recontextualizes the entire film on rewatch. Great sci-fi asks real questions.

title: The Banshees of Inisherin
notes: A movie about a friendship ending that somehow feels deeply political. Beautifully bleak.

title: Mad Max: Fury Road
notes: The greatest action movie ever made. Two hours of pure momentum. Furiosa carries everything.

title: Annihilation
notes: Doesn't explain itself and is better for it. Deeply weird. The lighthouse sequence is haunting.

title: Clueless
notes: Holds up completely. Sharper than it gets credit for. A genuine comfort rewatch.

title: The Father
notes: Anthony Hopkins gives the performance of his career. Structurally brilliant — puts you inside dementia.

title: Dune
notes: The best adaptation of the book we could have hoped for. Visually staggering. Immediately wanted part 2.

title: Moonlight
notes: Quiet and shattering. One of those films that changes how you see things after.
